explanation:
**Nutrition and fitness** rely on physical principles (energy balance) and biological factors (macronutrients, micronutrients). This calculator uses formulas validated by the scientific community (ACSM, ISSN, NIH).

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the Mifflin-St Jeor TDEE formula using your input values, checking for typical ranges and alerting you if values fall outside normal expectations.

## Standard Caloric Distribution

| Macronutrient | % of Calories | kcal per gram |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15-35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45-65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fat | 20-35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25-38g/day | — |

## When to Consult a Nutritionist

- If you have medical conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring personalized nutrition.
- If you want to gain significant muscle mass or compete in sports.
- If you struggle with disordered eating (binge eating, extreme restriction).
- If you haven't made progress toward your goal after 3 months.
- To plan complete vegan or vegetarian nutrition.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What is the Mifflin-St Jeor formula based on?
faq.a: The Mifflin-St Jeor formula is grounded in peer-reviewed research and official guidelines current through 2026.

faq.q: Are these results personalized to me?
faq.a: These calculations provide population averages. You should adjust them based on your individual circumstances, age, activity level, and personal response.

faq.q: Should I consult a doctor before making changes?
faq.a: Always consult your doctor before making significant dietary changes or starting supplements.
All information reflects 2026 research and the latest scientific studies available.

faq.q: How many calories should I eat per day?
faq.a: It depends on age, sex, weight, and activity level. Generally: adult women need 1,800–2,200 kcal, men need 2,200–3,000 kcal. Use this TDEE calculator for a precise estimate based on your metrics.

faq.q: What's a safe rate for weight loss?
faq.a: Healthy weight loss is 0.5–1 kg per week. Faster rates cause muscle loss and rebound weight gain. Sustainable progress is slower and more consistent.

faq.q: How much protein do I need daily?
faq.a: Sedentary individuals: 0.8g per kg of body weight. Active people: 1.2–1.5g/kg. Muscle building: 1.6–2.2g/kg of lean body mass. The effective upper limit is around 2.2g/kg.
Creatine and whey protein have strong scientific evidence. BCAAs and multivitamins have weaker evidence. Always prioritize a solid diet over supplementation.

faq.q: How do I adjust this calculator for my lifestyle?
faq.a: Use your baseline TDEE as a starting point. If you're not seeing results after 2–3 weeks, adjust your calorie intake by 100–200 calories and monitor changes.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
